title: Venezuela Official/Black-Market Bolivar-Dollar Exchange Rate Milestones (2003-2020) -- Compiled
source_org: Compiled by this project from multiple secondary/academic sources (curated, hand-compiled -- see docs/bookkeeping.md rule 5)
time_coverage: 2003-2020 (sparse dated milestones, not a continuous daily/monthly series)
units: VEB/VEF/VES per USD (three successive currency units across two redenominations -- 2008 VEB->VEF at 1000:1, 2018 VEF->VES at 100000:1; EACH ROW IS IN THE UNIT STATED AT THAT DATE, deliberately NOT rebased to a single common unit, to avoid introducing conversion errors -- a future user doing cross-period comparison must apply the redenomination factors explicitly)
license_terms: compiled from multiple sources cited per-row
notes: COMPARATOR dataset for the parallel-FX mission (Venezuela explicitly named in the mission brief as a comparator with 'well-documented parallel/black-market FX premium history'). This is a genuine best-effort result, not a full clean continuous series: attempted to get a real continuous daily/monthly Venezuela parallel-rate series comparable to what was found for Argentina (ArgentinaDatos API) or Iran (TGJU), but hit dead ends -- pydolarve.org (a documented open API with historical-price endpoints) is UNREACHABLE from this environment (DNS/connection failure via curl, WebFetch, AND the browser tool's in-page fetch -- confirmed genuinely down/blocked, not a one-off network blip, tried 3 different methods); the old dolartoday.com S3 JSON feed is confirmed DEAD (multiple sources note replacement scrapers exist because the original endpoint disappeared); Malone & Ter Horst's academic paper 'The Black Market for Dollars in Venezuela' (hosted at banrep.gov.co, the Colombian central bank -- a legitimate, reachable host) turned out to be IMAGE-based (scanned figures/tables embedded as JPEGs within the PDF, not machine-readable text), so its Feb-2003-to-Aug-2008 monthly data could not be extracted without a full visual-transcription pass (flagged as a possible future addition using this project's established pdftoppm-plus-visual-read methodology, not attempted this round due to time budget). What WAS successfully compiled: 21 dated milestone rows spanning three currency redenominations (Bolivar Fuerte VEF pre-2018, Bolivar Soberano VES post-2018), covering the CADIVI (2003) / CENCOEX+SICAD-I+SIMADI (2015 three-tier system) / DICOM (2018) succession of official-rate regimes alongside contemporaneous black-market rates at the same dates -- enough to see the ORDER OF MAGNITUDE of Venezuela's premium blow-out (official 6.3 vs black-market ~1,000 VEF/USD by Feb 2016; official DICOM 63.81 vs black-market 171.34 VES/USD by Oct 2018) even without a smooth interpolatable series. Cross-referenced against the project's existing data/raw/ovf-venezuela/ and data/raw/bcv-venezuela/ folders -- those cover INFLATION/economic-activity bulletins (OVF) and presumably official BCV rates (not independently re-verified this round), neither is a parallel-FX time series, so this fills a genuinely distinct gap rather than duplicating.

failures.reason: DNS/connection failure (getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND) via direct curl; also failed via WebFetch (navigation denied) and via browser in-page fetch() (CORS/Failed to fetch) -- domain appears genuinely unreachable from this environment across three independent methods
Confirmed dead by multiple independent secondary sources (original DolarToday S3 JSON feed discontinued) -- not attempted directly given corroborating evidence

PDF fetched successfully (212KB) but its data tables/charts are embedded as non-text JPEG images, not extractable via standard text tools -- would need the project's visual pdftoppm-transcription methodology to recover, not attempted this round
